commit.message:
feat: real tool loop in AgentHarness::run() (Phase 1b)

AgentHarness::run() now implements a proper agent loop:
- Calls LLM via provider.chat() (non-streaming, gets tool_calls)
- Checks stop_reason: EndTurn → return text, ToolUse → execute tools
- Executes tools via ToolRegistry, feeds results back to history
- Loops until final text response or max_tool_rounds exhausted
- LoopDetector: detects repeated tool calls with same args (threshold 3)
- Circuit breaker: 5 consecutive tool failures → stop with explanation
- CancellationToken checked at each iteration
- TokenBudget warnings emitted at 70%/85%/95%
- Events: tool_call_start, tool_result_end, text_delta, turn_complete
- Tool specs from ToolRegistry injected into ChatRequest.tools
- Proper tool result → history message with tool_use_id
- All 1,079 tests pass (6 test mocks updated for chat() vs stream())

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: improve card header readability

- Remove uppercase + tracking-tight from all card titles
- Switch from font-bold/font-black to font-semibold for body cards
- Increase font sizes slightly (12-13px -> 13-14px, sm:text-sm)
- Use line-clamp instead of truncate for better multi-line flow
- NEWS hero: text-2xl -> text-xl, font-black -> font-bold
- Featured blog: font-black -> font-bold, remove uppercase

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

refactor: replace flat application singleton with composable subsystem architecture

Redesign src/app/ from a plain object into an extensible Application<SS> class
that accepts typed subsystems as constructor arguments.

Core changes:
- AppCore.ts: generic Application<SS> class + createApplication factory;
  adds top-level reactive role() / setRole() signal for system-level access
  control (PASSENGER / GUEST / USER+)
- subsystems/types.ts: IAppSubsystem<AppT> interface + AppBase with role API
- AccountSubsystem: merged user+account session management; login/logout/asGuest
  push role changes to Application via init(app); AppUser carries no role field
- ApiSchemaSubsystem: field-mapping encode/decode with remote-load support
- BridgeSubsystem: hybrid JSBridge RPC for iOS/Android WebView host
- ReportSubsystem: global error capture + batched analytics tracking
- SocketSubsystem: named socket connection manager; accepts a single ISocket
  or a Record<string,ISocket>; default connection name is 'main'; supports
  runtime add/remove and convenience send/on/off delegators

Call-site updates:
- PageGuard, useAuthenticate: application.role() replaces account.maxRole()
- request.config: application.role(), account.current()
- Auth.index: account.login()
- ABTest: account.group()

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

System headers like bits/string_fortified.h re-declare memcpy/memmove/memset

redefinitions of l_memcpy, conflicting with l_os.h's static inline versions.

commit.message:
Fix interleave: replace broken single-pass swap with two-bucket merge

The old interleave() walked left-to-right and swapped the 3rd consecutive
same-article word with the nearest different one ahead. This produced a
predictable 'de de het de de het' repeating pattern instead of random
distribution.

New approach:
- Split words into separate de/het buckets, shuffle each independently
- Merge with a weighted coin flip (proportional to remaining bucket sizes)
- Hard constraint: max 2 consecutive same-article words
- Graceful fallback when one bucket runs out (unavoidable with 270 de vs 188 het)

Tested: 100 trials of full array, max run = 2 for first ~376 words.
Only 'All words' mode can exceed 2 at the tail when het words are exhausted.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

devices, organized across 9 threat categories:

- Credential Theft (6): zANTI, Xposed, SuperSU, Magisk, PCAPdroid
- Unauthorized Cloud Storage (6): Dropbox, MEGA, ProtonMail, Briar
- Cracked/Piracy (5): HappyMod, Aptoide, Lucky Patcher
- Crypto Mining (3): Trust Wallet, Coinbase, Blockchain.com
- Social Engineering (3): TextNow, OnionShare, Wire
- Device Tampering (4): KingRoot, KingoRoot, Island, Superuser

Each entry includes real package names, threat level, risk description,
CVE references, network risks, and remediation steps.

Device scanning via ADB cross-references installed apps against catalog,
calculates risk scores, and supports policy enforcement (alert, force-stop,
uninstall, quarantine).
